title: Error Classes, Discriminated Results, Causes, Retry, and Session Recovery · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript Pokedex interaction contains one plausible readline, token, type, validation, URL, fetch, promise, cache, abort, persistence, security, test, package, or release defect; find the first runtime or compile-time boundary failure before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in causal typed recovery decision model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Treat caught values as unknown and normalize Error, DOMException, abort reasons, and non-Error throws at one boundary.
Represent input, not-found, HTTP, network, timeout, abort, validation, invariant, storage, and internal failures as exhaustive variants.
Retain safe causal chains and operation identity while separating internal diagnostics from user-facing recovery text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using return and recoverable plus specific case evidence.
